title: Starter procedural character generator (humanoid-only)
intent: Replace Kaboom Crew's static player.prefab.json + bot.prefab.json mesh paths with a deterministic procedural character generator. A `CharacterRecipe` (seed + archetype + proportions + palette + accessories) goes in, a node-tree of primitive meshes (torso + head + armL + armR + legL + legR + accessory nodes) parented under the bomber entity comes out. Same seed → same character, always. Humanoid is the only archetype in this story; the recipe schema reserves `drone` / `puffball` enum values but the generator does not produce them yet. No animation in scope here — this story builds the node tree that GDP-009 will animate.
rationale: Source: `docs/game-design/characters-and-visual.md` §2 (the pipeline shape) + §6.1 (humanoid-only decision). The user-locked decision on 2026-05-20: procedural mesh + procedural animation, no Mixamo / authored GLB humanoids. The generator is the gating piece — without a node tree, GDP-009 has nothing to mutate. Humanoid-only is the minimum-viable expansion of the current single-capsule prefab and unblocks every later character work (animation pack, vocal synth seed, cosmetic unlocks).
acceptanceHints: NEW epic: KABOOM-CREW-CHARACTERS that ties together GDP-008 / GDP-009 / GDP-010 and any future archetype additions. The KABOOM-CREW-MVP-2 epic stays focused on gameplay (Kick / Remote / bot personalities).

schemas/components/character-recipe.schema.json (or examples/kaboom-crew/schemas/scene-extensions): CharacterRecipe with fields { seed:integer, archetype:'humanoid'|'drone'|'puffball', proportions:{torsoHeight,headSize,legLength,armLength all 0.7..1.3}, palette:{primary,secondary,accent}?, accessories:string[] (0..3 from the accessory enum) }. archetype defaults to 'humanoid'; non-humanoid values throw 'archetype not yet implemented' from the generator.

Generator module under examples/kaboom-crew/src/characters/ exposes generateHumanoid(recipe) → { root: Object3D, nodes: { torso, head, armL, armR, legL, legR, accessories[] } } using Three.js primitives (CapsuleGeometry, SphereGeometry, CylinderGeometry). All node-creation parameters derive deterministically from the recipe seed via a small XOR-shift RNG — no Math.random() anywhere.

Palette derivation: if recipe.palette is absent, the generator picks primary from a fixed 8-hue ring (HSL h=0/45/90/135/180/225/270/315, s=70%, l=50%) using `seed % 8`; secondary is the complement (h+180); accent is fixed cyan #33ffff. The 8-hue selection table is shared with the minimap-marker colour and the per-bomber blast-particle colour.
Accessory enum: ['antennae', 'visor', 'backpack', 'cap', 'fins']. Generator picks 0..3 deterministic from seed. Each accessory has a single primitive composition function — no external mesh files.

Spawn integration: examples/kaboom-crew/bootstrap.ts loads player.1 / bot.1 prefabs as before for ECS components, then REPLACES the MeshRenderer mesh with the generator output node tree under a child Object3D. The prefab's Transform / GridPosition / GridMover / BomberStats stay authoritative; the generator only owns visuals.

Recipe sources, in priority order: (1) `?recipe=<base64-json>` URL param applied to player.1 (DEBUG / AGENT only, not advertised in UI). (2) `?botRecipe=<base64-json>` for bot.1. (3) Fallback recipes — player.1 uses seed=1, bot.1 uses seed=2 — so the default experience still gets two visually distinct bombers without any URL.

Agent surface: window.__agf.kaboom.spawnWithRecipe(entityId, recipe) — accepts a CharacterRecipe object, replaces that entity's mesh node tree with the new generator output. Returns Promise<void> that resolves once the swap is complete.
Acceptance test: load with default URL → player.1 and bot.1 visibly distinct silhouettes + distinct primary hues; both have valid GridPosition / BomberStats / minimap marker.

Acceptance test: load with `?recipe=<base64 of {seed:42}>` → player.1 silhouette is deterministically the one for seed=42; reloading the same URL produces an IDENTICAL mesh tree (compared structurally via node names + Transform values).
Acceptance test: call `__agf.kaboom.spawnWithRecipe('bot.1', { seed: 99, archetype:'humanoid' })` mid-round → bot.1's visuals swap, gameplay state (alive, BomberStats, GridPosition) is preserved.
Acceptance test: call the same probe with archetype='drone' → rejects with a clear error mentioning 'not yet implemented'.

Unit tests: deterministic-by-seed (two calls with same recipe produce structurally identical trees), palette ring (seeds 0..7 produce 8 distinct primary hues), accessory cardinality (always 0..3 accessories, no duplicates).

HUD impact: minimap marker colour switches from hard-coded player-blue / bot-red to recipe primary colour. This is the one place the generator's existence is visible to the player without explicit setup.

Out of scope (explicit): animation systems (GDP-009), vocal synth (GDP-010), drone/puffball archetypes, cosmetic-unlock-driven accessory gating, recipe migration, save/load of recipes to persistent profile.
